title: Fix loadGraphData crash on malformed JSON and deduplicate impact hints
description:
Two bugs found in src/core/graph.ts during audit:

1. **loadGraphData crashes on malformed JSON** — Line 104: `JSON.parse(readFileSync(...))` is not wrapped in try/catch. If graph.json exists but contains invalid JSON, buildBrief crashes instead of degrading gracefully. This violates the spec requirement that briefs should always succeed even when graph data is missing or invalid.

2. **findImpactHints produces duplicate hints** — When multiple path_hints match the same node (e.g. "src/core/brief.ts" and "src/core/"), the inbound/outbound processing runs twice for that node, adding identical hint strings. Need to track processed nodes to avoid duplicates.

Plus two dead-code items:
- graph_data_invalid in result.ts:215 is never referenced (can remove or use it)
- !graph.concepts check in findRelevantConcepts is always false after remapGraph (concepts is always an array)
acceptance: loadGraphData returns empty graph when graph.json contains invalid JSON (no crash).
findImpactHints does not produce duplicate hint strings when multiple path_hints match the same node.
Add test: loadGraphData handles malformed JSON gracefully.
Add test: findImpactHints deduplicates when path_hints overlap.
typecheck, biome check, and waystation validate all clean.
notes: Found during audit of commit 95f0fcb. The crash bug is critical because it breaks the graceful degradation contract. The duplicate hints bug is a quality issue that makes brief output noisy.
